l0-w1-d1-shadow.text: Pat put the pen in the paper bag. Bob bought a big blue ball.

l0-w1-d2-shadow.text: Today I study at the table. The teacher told us ten new words.

l0-w1-d3-shadow.text: I came to class. The game was good. Can I get a cup of coffee?

l0-w1-d4-shadow.text: I sit in my seat and eat. Please leave — I need to live here.

l0-w1-d5-shadow.text: My name is ___. Today is a bad day because I am late. Can you take my bag?

l0-w1-d6-shadow.text: I practice my pronunciation every day. Today I can say these sounds better than yesterday. Pat, bat. Sheep, ship. Late, cat. I am getting better!

l0-w1-d7-shadow.text: Good morning. My name is ___. I am happy to be here today. I can see ten people in the big class. Please sit in your seat. The teacher is late but that is okay. I will eat my breakfast — bread and tea. This city is nice. I like it.

l0-w2-d1-shadow.text: I have five friends. Every Friday we visit my favorite place. I love it very much.

l0-w2-d2-shadow.text: I think three things: this, that, and the other. My mother thanks them both.

l0-w2-d3-shadow.text: This is easy. His sister has six prizes. Please close your eyes because it is a surprise.

l0-w2-d4-shadow.text: My father has an old car. I don't know if he is home. Go to the park — it's not far. I hope it's not too hot or cold.

l0-w2-d5-shadow.text: I put the book on the table and look at the food. It's good. The room is full. I should go to school soon — it's too far to walk.

l0-w2-d6-shadow.text: I think this food is very good. My father has five books — they are full of very useful things. I never visit the school on Thursday because it is too far.

l0-w2-d7-shadow.text: My father thinks this food is very good. He has five favorite things: his family, his car, good books, cold weather, and football on Thursday. Three of us go to school every day. The room is not very full — there is food for everyone. I am very thankful for everything.

l0-w3-d1-shadow.text: Good morning. Something interesting is happening this evening. I am running — nothing can stop me. I am getting stronger and stronger.

l0-w3-d2-shadow.text: Hello! How are you today? I want to go home with you. We worked hard this week. Yes, you are young and happy.

l0-w3-d3-shadow.text: Today I want to go to a computer store. My family and I — we live together. About a hundred people support us. It's not a problem.

l0-w3-d4-shadow.text: I work early in the morning. First, I learn some new words. My mother and brother come from another country. I love them very much — it's a wonderful world.

l0-w3-d5-shadow.text: I get up and get ready. Then I eat breakfast with my friend at seven. He said he never gets enough rest. I set ten things on the desk every morning. It's getting better.

l0-w3-d6-shadow.text: This morning I went to work early. I learned something new about computers. My friend and brother were together — they said nothing about the problem. Everything is getting better. I never give up.

l0-w3-d7-shadow.text: Every morning I learn something new about the world. My mother and brother work together at a computer company. Nothing about it is a problem for her — she is wonderful. I want to get better at everything. First, I turn on my computer. Then I work for about seven hours. In the evening, my family comes together. We never forget to support each other.

l0-w4-d1-shadow.text: I really like learning languages. The road is long but I love the ride. I look at the light on the right. I read and I listen. I learn and I grow.

l0-w4-d2-shadow.text: I feel really good this morning. I arrived here early. The world looks beautiful after the rain. I want to call my family. All of them are far from here. I live near the school. The water is cold but I don't care.

l0-w4-d3-shadow.text: I just changed my job in June. My teacher is rich and generous. I enjoy watching the children choose lunch. Each age brings a new challenge. Which language do you like?

l0-w4-d4-shadow.text: I like my life right now. Five times I tried to find my voice. The boy pointed down the road. How about we go out? I enjoy the sound of the night. It's time to join.

l0-w4-d5-shadow.text: I ride my bike around town. I turn right at the light. The road is long but I like it. The loud boys are playing outside. I enjoy this time — night time is the right time. I am loyal to my choice.

l0-w4-d6-shadow.text: I really love my life right now. Learning English is my choice. I enjoy riding around town — the roads are long but beautiful. Last night I joined my friends. The children were playing loudly. It was really nice.

l0-w4-d7-shadow.text: I really like my life right now. I live in a large house near a beautiful river. Every morning I ride my bike around the town. I look right, then left. The road is long but I enjoy the ride. Last night I joined my friends at a restaurant. The children were playing loudly. We had a choice: watch a movie or listen to music. I chose to listen. The royal concert was loud and joyful. Learning English is my choice — I love it.

l0-w5-d1-shadow.text: I am here. I can hear you clearly. This year is nearly over. I have no fear — my career path is clear. Come near and listen, dear.

l0-w5-d2-shadow.text: Where is my chair? Over there, in the fresh air. I prepared to share my work. It's only fair. I don't care about my hair. I am aware of what's there.

l0-w5-d3-shadow.text: I parked my car far from the apartment. It was dark but not hard to find. I walked through the garden to the market. The stars were beautiful. My heart felt happy. This is the start of something wonderful.

l0-w5-d4-shadow.text: This year I started a new career. I heard about it at a party — my friend shared the news. I wasn't afraid to start far from here. Where I work now, the air is clear and the garden is dark and beautiful. I care about my heart and my art.

l0-w5-d5-shadow.text: I run every morning. The road is really long — three streets from here. My friend drives me around in her car. I'm sorry I arrived late. During breakfast I remembered: the restaurant is on the right after the bridge. It was a strong, rainy morning.

l0-w5-d6-shadow.text: I started my career near here last year. The market is far from my apartment but I don't care. Every morning I prepare, start the car, and drive through the dark streets. The air is clear and I can hear the birds.

l0-w5-d7-shadow.text: Every year I hear the same story: learning English is hard. I don't agree. The truth is clear — if you start, if you care, if you practice, you get better. My friend started last year with fear in her heart. She could barely share a word. Now? She reads, she speaks, she drives conversations forward. Her career is far from where it started. She is near her dream. The air is different here — full of opportunity. Don't stare at the mountain — start climbing.

l1-w1-d1-shadow.text: I will PREsent my PROject, and then I will reCORD the meeting on my new REcord player.

l1-w1-d2-shadow.text: I want to go to the store to buy a present for my friend.

l1-w1-d3-shadow.text: I want some coffee this morning because I can't find my keys anywhere, and we're going to a party tonight.

l1-w1-d4-shadow.text: Every MORNing, I open the WINdow in my KITchen and feel HAPpy looking at the YELlow flowers in the GARden.

l1-w1-d5-shadow.text: In my BEDroom, I keep my TOOTHbrush and SUNglasses near the CLASSroom door, not far from the old GREENhouse.

l1-w1-d6-shadow.text: Every week I try to record my progress. My record shows I present my project better each time I present it — and that makes me happy.

l1-w1-d7-shadow.text: Every morning, I present my daily plan to my team. I object when people don't prepare, because a good project needs a good record of what happened before. Yesterday, my present for my brother was a record player — an old one, but he was happy to receive it in his bedroom.

l1-w2-d1-shadow.text: I bought some butter and water at the party in the city — it's a pretty little place.

l1-w2-d2-shadow.text: My buddy is ready to study for the wedding — his body is muddy from climbing that old ladder.

l1-w2-d3-shadow.text: What about getting up early? There's a lot of work, and not at all of it is easy — but get up anyway.

l1-w2-d4-shadow.text: Press the button, drink some water, and climb the little mountain — no matter what is written, it's better than staying home.

l1-w2-d5-shadow.text: It doesn't matter what city — a little water and butter make everything better, and studying a little every day gets you a lot further.

l1-w2-d6-shadow.text: Every day my buddy studies a little better. He's a writer who likes to get up early, put water and butter on the table, and climb a muddy ladder — better than the mountain, no matter what.

l1-w2-d7-shadow.text: Every Saturday, my buddy and I get up early and go to the city to study at a little cafe. The water there is better than at home, and the butter on the bread is amazing. Last week, a writer sat next to us — he was working on a book about a mountain village. It doesn't matter what day it is; a little coffee and a lot of conversation always make it a good morning.

l1-w3-d1-shadow.text: Pick up an apple, look at it, and put it in an hour — then turn off the light and get up.

l1-w3-d2-shadow.text: This is easy — give it to one of us, and have a seat, because as usual, I love it.

l1-w3-d3-shadow.text: Stop it — that's a big idea, not at all bad. Look up, took a picture, in black and white.

l1-w3-d4-shadow.text: Come on in, wait a minute — what about it? Hang on a second and check it out.

l1-w3-d5-shadow.text: I picked up an apple, ate it all up, turned off the light, and gave it a rest — look at us, we made it out okay.

l1-w3-d6-shadow.text: Come on in, have a seat — pick up an apple, give it a look, and wait a minute. This is easy, not at all a big idea, but check it out anyway.

l1-w3-d7-shadow.text: Every evening, I pick up an apple on my way home and eat it all up before dinner. It's not at all a big idea, but it helps me relax. Come on in, have a seat, and give it a try yourself — turn off your phone for an hour and just enjoy a quiet moment. Look at us now, we made it out of a busy day okay.

l1-w4-d1-shadow.text: It's important — press the button, and I'm certain the kitten's name is written on a cotton sentence card, near the mountain.

l1-w4-d2-shadow.text: It doesn't matter — I'm certain the water is better near the mountain, and it's written on a little button by the trail.

l1-w4-d3-shadow.text: Pay attention — it's important to draw the curtain, walk to the fountain, and be certain about every sentence near the mountain.

l1-w4-d4-shadow.text: The button on the water bottle matters — I'm certain the little kitten is better off near the mountain than in the city.

l1-w4-d5-shadow.text: It's certain and important — press that button now, near the fountain, under the mountain curtain, exactly as it's written.

l1-w4-d6-shadow.text: It's certain and important: press the button near the mountain fountain. It doesn't matter what's written on the curtain — the little kitten already knows it's better.

l1-w4-d7-shadow.text: It's important to know a mountain trail before you climb it. Press the button on your water bottle, draw back the curtain of morning fog, and walk toward the fountain at the base. I'm certain the view gets better with every step — the little kitten that lives near the visitor center is written about in every guidebook. No matter the weather, this mountain matters to everyone in the city.

l1-w5-d1-shadow.text: Let's go out — who is coming? Do it now, it's too easy, so go away and let me finish with my blue eyes on the clock.

l1-w5-d2-shadow.text: She asked if I am taking a day off, but we are going to the office with my aunt anyway.

l1-w5-d3-shadow.text: She asked who is going out today — I am taking a day off, and it's too easy to just go out and enjoy my blue eyes in the sun.

l1-w5-d4-shadow.text: Go away, I am busy — she asked who is here, but it's too old a question, and I am too easy to please anyway.

l1-w5-d5-shadow.text: Just go out already — who is stopping you? I am too easy to please, and we are taking a day off because it's too easy to enjoy.

l1-w5-d6-shadow.text: She asked who is going out today. I am too easy to say no — go away, we are taking a day off, and it's too easy to enjoy the blue sky.

l1-w5-d7-shadow.text: She asked if I am free to go out today, and I am! Who is coming with us? We are meeting my aunt at the office first, then we'll go away to the lake — it's too easy to say no to a day off like this. The blue sky and blue eyes of summer make everything feel new again.

l1-w6-d1-shadow.text: Tell him to call her, give him the book, and ask her too — I went with him, not for her.

l1-w6-d2-shadow.text: I have been waiting since she has gone — we had left before you have seen it, and he has told them what they had known.

l1-w6-d3-shadow.text: Him? He never called her. I have a car, but I have been walking lately — and I told HER the news, not him.

l1-w6-d4-shadow.text: Did you tell him? Could you ask her too? I should have known, and I would have called him and given her a call about the plan.

l1-w6-d5-shadow.text: Just tell him already! I have been busy, and she has gone home — did you ask her, or would you have called him yourself?

l1-w6-d6-shadow.text: Him? I never told him. I have been meaning to ask her, but she has gone already — did you give him the news, or should I have told her myself?

l1-w6-d7-shadow.text: Him? He never called her back. I told him to ask her about the meeting, but he has been too busy. She has gone to the office already, and I would have joined her if I have had more time. Did you tell him the schedule changed? I have a feeling he hasn't heard yet — could you give him a call and ask her to wait?

l1-w7-d1-shadow.text: Good day! It's a big game today — have some milk and black coffee before you stop at that red door.

l1-w7-d2-shadow.text: What time is it? Last time, we had a good time right there — it's a big deal, that time especially.

l1-w7-d3-shadow.text: We stayed ten nights in November and wanted some more — just give me one minute, ten nights isn't enough.

l1-w7-d4-shadow.text: That time, next Tuesday, my big brother is bringing some snacks — good grief, write it on the black board so we remember!

l1-w7-d5-shadow.text: What a big game that was! What time did it end? I remember some milk, ten nights, that time — next Tuesday's game will be even better.

l1-w7-d6-shadow.text: What a big game! Some milk and black coffee made it a good time. Ten nights in November, next Tuesday's game, and my big brother bringing some snacks — good grief, what a time that was.

l1-w7-d7-shadow.text: What a big game that was last Tuesday! We had some milk and black coffee before it started, and my big brother stayed ten nights just to watch it live. What time does the next one start? Good grief, I need some more coffee — that time flew by so fast, and next Tuesday's rematch will be an even bigger deal.

l1-w8-d1-shadow.text: Tell me about your trip again — we walked along and around the park, and then she went away, alone, above the shop.

l1-w8-d2-shadow.text: My teacher is also a doctor — the water needs a little sugar, no matter the color, because the actor was better than his brother.

l1-w8-d3-shadow.text: I need a book for the class — she is taller than her brother, as I said, and this is one of the best gifts of all.

l1-w8-d4-shadow.text: My family loves chocolate and vegetables — this camera is comfortable and different from mine, so bring it for a family photo.

l1-w8-d5-shadow.text: Tell me about the teacher and the camera again — my family's doctor loves chocolate, as I said, and the camera is a gift for the family, about time too!

l1-w8-d6-shadow.text: About a family camera, my teacher and the doctor talked again — the chocolate was different, the sofa was comfortable, and I told the teacher about the vegetable garden and the family photo.

l1-w8-d7-shadow.text: About a month ago, my family bought a new camera for our vacation. My teacher told us again about the difference between a comfortable trip and a difficult one, so we planned around the schedule of a local doctor who knows the area. We brought chocolate and vegetables for the family, and everyone had a wonderful time together, again and again.

l1-w9-d1-shadow.text: About the camera — the teacher is away again, and I forgot a family photo, so I forgot about the whole thing.

l1-w9-d4-shadow.text: About an hour ago, I picked up an apple and some water near the big game. Did you tell him the button on the camera is broken? It's a big deal — ask her about the mountain trip again.

l1-w10-d7-shadow.text: About ten weeks ago, I started Level 1 as a beginner in connected speech. My teacher and I have been working on the water-and-butter flap, the button-and-mountain glottal stop, and linking words like pick up an apple and go out. I have also learned to tell him and ask her naturally, dropping the h, and to say about and camera with a proper schwa instead of a full vowel. It's a big deal, and I'm certain I'm better prepared for Level 2 now than I was before.

l2-w3-d1-shadow.text: The student read the news about the new tune — it's my duty to report the nuclear news, and the new duke changed his tune.

l2-w3-d3-shadow.text: The music was new, and it took only a few minutes to tune — that cute duke found a cure for his student's problem with a few new songs.

l2-w3-d5-shadow.text: This new opportunity at the institute is exactly what the student needs — I'd like to present the news about our new institute today, and tune my resume to fit it.

l2-w3-d6-shadow.text: The new student at the institute found a great opportunity, but it took a few minutes to tune his resume for the news interview — that cute duke's new music made it worth the duty.

l2-w3-d7-shadow.text: As a new student at this institute, I've had a few great opportunities already. My duty this week was to tune up my resume and present the latest news about our research. It's a cute coincidence, but the music program here is new too, and a few students told me it's one of the best in the country. I truly believe this institute constitutes a unique opportunity for anyone who wants a fresh start.

l2-w5-d1-shadow.text: I feel cool after the tall school bell rang — the ball fell well past the full pool near the tall, cool school.

l2-w5-d2-shadow.text: The little local shop is lovely and likely loyal to its customers — this level of service is a likely, local, lovely charm.

l2-w5-d3-shadow.text: Please help me find the milk and salt — the film's result was worth it for every adult who needed a belt fix.

l2-w7-d1-shadow.text: I think the bank needs a pink sink — thank you for the drink, I'll sink into this chair while my uncle sorts through the junk.

l2-w7-d2-shadow.text: My finger points at a single angle — England has an interesting language, and hunger made him point at the food.

l2-w7-d3-shadow.text: I think one cat is enough — my finger points at one goat near the bank, where I also saw one kite.

l2-w7-d4-shadow.text: Thanks for thinking about the English banking system — the singer is getting stronger and longer with practice, and I'm thinking about drinking less.

l2-w7-d5-shadow.text: When thinking analytically about English banking, we see stronger, longer-term trends — thanks to clear thinking, our strategy is stronger.

l2-w7-d6-shadow.text: I think one cat and one goat are enough — my finger points at the bank, where I'm thinking about English banking and a singer's stronger, longer performance.

l2-w7-d7-shadow.text: I'm thinking about my banking habits these days. Thanks to some careful thinking, I've realized that drinking less coffee and saving more could make my finances stronger.
